question_text: Light travels from water into air. What happens to the light as it crosses the boundary?

options.label: B
options.text: It bends away from the normal

options.label: C
options.text: It travels in a straight line

options.label: D
options.text: It bends at 90° to the normal

explanation: Light bends away from the normal when moving from a denser medium (water) to a less dense medium (air). This is because light travels faster in air than in water.
